project.title: Kryptos K4 Research Dashboard
project.tagline: A grounded view of the repository's hypotheses, benchmarks, and latest structured runs.

benchmarks.label: Python CPU sweep
benchmarks.notes: Repository baseline for the pre-compiled Python heuristics.

benchmarks.label: Mojo benchmark scaffold
benchmarks.notes: Compiled benchmark path; not presented as a full native K4 decryptor.

benchmarks.label: OpenCL GPU sweep
benchmarks.notes: Native OpenCL pass targeting an AMD Radeon 680M.

strategy_catalog.name: Quagmire III Running Keys
strategy_catalog.objective: Test whether the solved K1-K3 plaintexts can act as direct running keys for K4.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: K4 reuses the Kryptos mixed alphabet and one of the earlier panel plaintexts as its key material.

strategy_catalog.name: Spatial Matrix Reassembly
strategy_catalog.objective: Read padded 7x14 and 14x7 layouts in alternate orders to search for clue-bearing ciphertext runs.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: K4 may hide anchor substrings through simple geometric rearrangement before substitution.

strategy_catalog.name: Hybrid Transposition Search
strategy_catalog.objective: Use hill climbing with clue overlap and local IoC heuristics to rank candidate column orders.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: A clue-bearing transposition may surface when column order improves both anchor overlap and local English-like structure.

strategy_catalog.name: Quagmire III Autokey
strategy_catalog.objective: Test theme-relevant primers under plain-autokey and cipher-autokey Quagmire variants.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: K4 may bootstrap its own key stream from a short thematic primer.

strategy_catalog.name: Geometric Grilles
strategy_catalog.objective: Apply logical masks across a padded 7x14 grid to test simple grille-style extractions.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: A repeated mask pattern might expose the clue ciphertext as a contiguous extraction.

strategy_catalog.name: Chained Autokey
strategy_catalog.objective: Test double-layer Quagmire autokey combinations across theme-relevant primer pairs.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: K4 could be a nested autokey construction requiring two sequential decryptions.

strategy_catalog.name: Segmented Resets
strategy_catalog.objective: Split K4 on 'W' and retry running-key and autokey decryption with per-segment resets.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: The repeated 'W' characters may mark segment boundaries or key resets.

strategy_catalog.name: Shifted Running Keys
strategy_catalog.objective: Exhaustively test all offsets of K1-K3 plaintexts and the KRYPTOS primer as running key material.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: The right offset into prior Kryptos text may align K4 with a standard running-key construction.

strategy_catalog.name: External Text Running Key
strategy_catalog.objective: Slide a historical text window across Howard Carter diary excerpts as candidate running keys.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: An external text tied to the K3 clue may supply the real running key stream for K4.

strategy_catalog.name: Bifid Fractionation
strategy_catalog.objective: Test Polybius/Bifid-style fractionation across theme-derived keywords and multiple periods.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: K4 may flatten its statistics through coordinate fractionation before substitution.

results.name: Quagmire III Running Keys
results.objective: Test whether the solved K1-K3 plaintexts can act as direct running keys for K4.
results.hypothesis: K4 reuses the Kryptos mixed alphabet and one of the earlier panel plaintexts as its key material.
results.summary: No prior Kryptos plaintext produced the known K4 anchors as a direct running key.
notes: Checked K1, K2, and K3 plaintexts as direct repeating running keys.

results.name: Spatial Matrix Reassembly
results.objective: Read padded 7x14 and 14x7 layouts in alternate orders to search for clue-bearing ciphertext runs.
results.hypothesis: K4 may hide anchor substrings through simple geometric rearrangement before substitution.
results.summary: No simple 7x14 or 14x7 read order reconstructed the clue ciphertext as a contiguous run.
notes: Tested padded 7x14 and 14x7 matrices with column and diagonal reads.

results.name: Hybrid Transposition Search
results.objective: Use hill climbing with clue overlap and local IoC heuristics to rank candidate column orders.
results.hypothesis: A clue-bearing transposition may surface when column order improves both anchor overlap and local English-like structure.
results.summary: No known anchors surfaced; the best candidate came from width 7 with local IoC 0.1582.
notes: Fitness combines clue overlap with chunked IoC so the objective matches the documented heuristic.

results.name: Quagmire III Autokey
results.objective: Test theme-relevant primers under plain-autokey and cipher-autokey Quagmire variants.
results.hypothesis: K4 may bootstrap its own key stream from a short thematic primer.
results.summary: No plain-autokey or cipher-autokey primer produced the known K4 anchors.
notes: Evaluated 14 primers in both plain and cipher autokey modes.

results.name: Geometric Grilles
results.objective: Apply logical masks across a padded 7x14 grid to test simple grille-style extractions.
results.hypothesis: A repeated mask pattern might expose the clue ciphertext as a contiguous extraction.
results.summary: No tested grille mask extracted the clue ciphertext as a continuous sequence.
notes: Used simple modulus masks plus alternating-column masks over a padded 7x14 grid.

results.name: Chained Autokey
results.objective: Test double-layer Quagmire autokey combinations across theme-relevant primer pairs.
results.hypothesis: K4 could be a nested autokey construction requiring two sequential decryptions.
results.summary: No chained autokey primer pair surfaced the known K4 anchors.
notes: Evaluated 49 primer pairs across four mode combinations each.

results.name: Segmented Resets
results.objective: Split K4 on 'W' and retry running-key and autokey decryption with per-segment resets.
results.hypothesis: The repeated 'W' characters may mark segment boundaries or key resets.
results.summary: Resetting the cipher at 'W' boundaries did not recover the known anchors.

results.name: Shifted Running Keys
results.objective: Exhaustively test all offsets of K1-K3 plaintexts and the KRYPTOS primer as running key material.
results.hypothesis: The right offset into prior Kryptos text may align K4 with a standard running-key construction.
results.summary: No running-key offset over K1-K3 or KRYPTOS produced the known K4 anchors.
notes: Evaluated every cyclic offset for the K1-K3 plaintexts and the KRYPTOS primer under Quagmire and Vigenere rules.

results.name: External Text Running Key
results.objective: Slide a historical text window across Howard Carter diary excerpts as candidate running keys.
results.hypothesis: An external text tied to the K3 clue may supply the real running key stream for K4.
results.summary: Sliding a Howard Carter diary window across K4 did not recover the known anchors.
notes: Tested 1261 diary offsets under Quagmire and Vigenere rules.

results.name: Bifid Fractionation
results.objective: Test Polybius/Bifid-style fractionation across theme-derived keywords and multiple periods.
results.hypothesis: K4 may flatten its statistics through coordinate fractionation before substitution.
results.summary: No tested Bifid keyword/period pair produced the full known K4 anchors.
